title: carex canina subsp. collifera
intro: Carex canina subsp. collifera is a subspecies of sedge, a type of grass-like perennial plant belonging to the Carex genus, native to various parts of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Carex canina subsp. collifera include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
